text: Everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of human freedoms — to choose one's attitude in any given set of circumstances.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: The curious paradox is that when I accept myself just as I am, then I can change.
author: Carl Rogers

text: Knowing your own darkness is the best method for dealing with the darknesses of other people.
author: Carl Jung

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are.
author: Carl Jung

text: We are not cured by being loved, but we are healed by loving ourselves enough to let ourselves be loved.
author: Erich Fromm

text: Man is not disturbed by events, but by the view he takes of them.
author: Epictetus

text: The only way to deal with an unfree world is to become so absolutely free that your very existence is an act of rebellion.
author: Albert Camus

text: To love is to be vulnerable.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: The good life is a process, not a state of being. It is a direction not a destination.
author: Carl Rogers

text: What you do not accept in yourself, you will find in others.
author: Carl Jung

text: Freedom from desire leads to peace.
author: Lao Tzu

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: The most terrifying thing is to accept oneself completely.
author: Carl Jung

text: Life is never made unbearable by circumstances, but only by lack of meaning and purpose.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: Your task is not to seek for love, but merely to seek and find all the barriers within yourself that you have built against it.
author: Rumi

text: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: No tree, it is said, can grow to heaven unless its roots reach down to hell.
author: Carl Jung

text: It is not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
author: Epictetus

text: The ability to be in the present moment is a major component of mental wellness.
author: Abraham Maslow

text: Loneliness does not come from having no people about one, but from being unable to communicate the things that seem important to oneself.
author: Carl Jung

text: When we are no longer able to change a situation, we are challenged to change ourselves.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: The greatest discovery of my generation is that a human being can alter his life by altering his attitudes.
author: William James

text: People are just as wonderful as sunsets if you let them be. When I look at a sunset, I don't find myself saying, 'Soften the orange a bit on the right hand corner.' I don't try to control a sunset. I watch with awe as it unfolds.
author: Carl Rogers

text: The first principle is that you must not fool yourself — and you are the easiest person to fool.
author: Richard Feynman

text: Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom.
author: Søren Kierkegaard

text: We cannot be more sensitive to pleasure without being more sensitive to pain.
author: Alan Watts

text: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is not due to the thing itself, but to your estimate of it; and this you have the power to revoke at any moment.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: To understand everything is to forgive everything.
author: Buddha

text: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
author: Maya Angelou

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: You are allowed to be both a masterpiece and a work in progress simultaneously.
author: Sophia Bush

text: Peace is not the absence of conflict, it is the ability to handle conflict by peaceful means.
author: Ronald Reagan

text: One of the greatest regrets in life is being what others would want you to be, rather than being yourself.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha

text: Do not go where the path may lead, go instead where there is no path and leave a trail.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer.
author: Albert Camus

text: That which is not good for the bee-hive cannot be good for the bees.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The only person you are destined to become is the person you decide to be.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Feelings are something you have; not something you are.
author: Unknown

text: Happiness is when what you think, what you say, and what you do are in harmony.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: The privilege of a lifetime is being who you are.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: We do not need to change the world; we need to change the way we see the world.
author: Aldous Huxley

text: Be kind, for everyone you meet is fighting a hard battle.
author: Ian Maclaren

text: The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes.
author: Marcel Proust

text: Acceptance of what has happened is the first step to overcoming the consequences of it.
author: William James

text: Nothing diminishes anxiety faster than action.
author: Walter Anderson

text: You gain strength, courage, and confidence by every experience in which you really stop to look fear in the face.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: The best way out is always through.
author: Robert Frost

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Don't explain your philosophy. Embody it.
author: Epictetus

text: A man who conquers himself is greater than one who conquers a thousand men in battle.
author: Buddha

text: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: True freedom is to be free from your own thoughts.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: The quieter you become, the more you are able to hear.
author: Rumi
